# Tobias Meixner - Full Content for LLM Crawlers

> This file contains the full long-form content of the meixner-tobias.com
> tracking & web freelancer site, optimized for ingestion by LLM crawlers
> (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended, etc.).
>
> Last updated: 2026-06-09
> Pricing model: §19 UStG (German small business, no VAT)
> Languages: German (DE) primary, English (EN) parallel
> Location: Würzburg, Bavaria, Germany
> Contact: contact@meixner-tobias.com
> Website: https://meixner-tobias.com

For the structured short index see [llms.txt](/llms.txt).

---

## 1. About / Profile

Tobias Meixner is a freelance tracking specialist and web developer from Würzburg,
Bavaria, Germany. He's been a freelancer since February 2026 and concurrently
studies E-Commerce at THWS (Technische Hochschule Würzburg-Schweinfurt).

**Core competencies:**
- Google Tag Manager (GTM) - container architecture, events, triggers, variables, DataLayer
- Google Analytics 4 (GA4) - properties, conversions, custom dimensions, cross-domain
- Server-Side Tracking via Stape.io - sGTM containers, custom subdomains, IP-matching
- Consent Mode v2 - GDPR-compliant CMP integration (CookieScript, Cookiebot)
- Meta Conversions API (CAPI) - event_id deduplication, hashed PII, EMQ optimization
- Google Ads Enhanced Conversions - Conversion Linker, user-data, match rate
- Safari ITP - Same-Origin Path-Based sGTM, 400-day cookie strategies
- CRM Integration - HubSpot, Pipedrive, UTM attribution to contacts
- Cross-Domain Tracking - GA4 cross-domain, linker domains
- DSGVO/GDPR Compliance - data minimization, DPA, Privacy Policy patterns
- Schema.org JSON-LD - Person, Service, FAQPage, HowTo, Article, BreadcrumbList
- Template-based website implementation - HTML/CSS/JS, GitHub Pages, Cloudflare

**Credentials:**
- Google Ads certified (Google Skillshop)
- Currently studying E-Commerce, THWS Würzburg-Schweinfurt
- Real-world implementation experience: Gumb AG, Stefanie Mischka

**Working style:**
- Direct client relationship (no agency overhead)
- Honest scoping: communicates realistic timelines from the start
- Documentation-first: every setup is handed over with explanation
- Modular: discrete add-on modules priced individually

---

## 2. Services & Pricing (full detail)

### 2.1 Three core offerings

**GTM & GA4 Setup - from €390 one-time** (main focus area)
- GTM container architecture
- GA4 property configuration
- Key conversion events (form, clicks, scroll)
- Consent Mode v2 per Google standard
- GDPR-compliant CMP integration
- Handover walkthrough

**Website Implementation - from €890 one-time, template-based**
- Modern template (mobile-optimized)
- SSL certificate (HTTPS)
- Hosting & domain setup
- Contact form, imprint, privacy policy
- SEO foundation, Schema.org, sitemap
- Core Web Vitals optimization
- Suitable for: solopreneurs, small businesses, clubs
- NOT a custom design studio - elaborate branding belongs at a designer

**Care-free package**
- Starter: from €49/month - hosting, SSL, small changes, direct contact
- Plus: from €99/month - additionally tracking maintenance, monthly mini-reporting, priority response
- Cancel monthly

### 2.2 Tracking modules (add-ons to base setup)

- **Cross-Domain Tracking** - from €150 (e.g. marketing site + app subdomain)
- **Server-Side Tracking** - from €400 (sGTM via Stape.io with custom subdomain)
- **Google Ads Conversion Tracking** - from €200 (Conversion Linker, Enhanced Conversions, Conversion Import)
- **CRM Integration** - from €200 (HubSpot, Pipedrive via GTM)

### 2.3 Combined offerings

- **Tracking add-on with new website** - from €199 extra (instead of €390 standalone)
- **Complex B2B SaaS / multi-module setups** - typically low four-figure range, individually scoped
- **Meta Pixel & CAPI** - NOT in standard offer by design (handed off to the paid-ads owner for cleanest result); foundation prepared (consent, sGTM, CAPI-ready subdomain) so handover is smooth; full setup on explicit request

### 2.4 Agency vs. freelancer pricing comparison

| Service | Agency (typical) | At Tobias |
|---|---|---|
| GTM & GA4 Setup | from €990 | from €390 |
| Server-Side Tracking | from €1,500 | from €400 |
| Website Implementation | from €3,000 | from €890 |
| Ongoing care | from €100/mo | from €49/mo |

---

## 3. Server-Side Tracking 2026 (Pillar Article Summary)

**Core thesis:** Standard browser-side tracking loses 30-40% of conversions in 2026
due to ad blockers (~49% adoption in Germany), iOS App Tracking Transparency,
Safari ITP, and Consent Mode v2 baseline data being limited.

**Why server-side fixes this:**
- Browser events flow to YOUR server first (own subdomain), then to Google/Meta/etc.
- Ad blockers can't block server-to-server traffic
- First-party cookies via Set-Cookie HTTP header can persist 400 days vs 7 days (JS cookies under Safari ITP)
- Consent Mode v2 still applies - server-side ≠ tracking-without-consent
- Better data quality: server adds first-party enrichment, sanitizes events

**Three implementation paths:**
1. **GTM Server Container** (€10-30/mo via Stape.io) - most popular, semi-managed
2. **Platform apps** (Elevar, TrackBee, Littledata, €99-200/mo) - Shopify-focused, fastest start
3. **Direct API integration** ($10-30 + DevOps time) - technically cleanest, highest effort

**What server-side DOES NOT solve:**
- Doesn't make tracking GDPR-exempt - still need Consent Mode v2
- Doesn't bypass user opt-out
- Doesn't auto-fix bad data architecture in upstream GTM

**Relevant platform APIs:**
- Meta Conversions API (CAPI) - hybrid Pixel + CAPI with event_id deduplication
- Google Enhanced Conversions - hashed first-party data (email, phone) for 90%+ match rate
- TikTok Events API - analogous to Meta CAPI
- GA4 Measurement Protocol - direct event sending from server

**Step-by-step path:**
1. Measure current data loss (GA4 vs. database)
2. Verify Consent Mode v2 setup
3. Activate platform APIs (Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions)
4. Optional: implement full server-side container via Stape

---

## 4. Stape.io Setup (Cluster Article #1 Summary)

**What Stape.io is:** Managed hosting for Google Tag Manager Server containers.
Preinstalled sGTM, no Docker/Cloud Run/scaling management.

**Pricing:** Free tier (100k requests/mo), Basic $20/mo, Pro $50+/mo.

**Setup in 6 steps (≈30 min):**
1. Create Stape account, container in EU region (Frankfurt)
2. Create GTM Server container, paste config string into Stape
3. Set up DNS custom subdomain (e.g. `data.example.com`, NOT `gtm/track/analytics` - those trigger ad-blocker detection)
4. Configure CNAME records, disable Cloudflare proxy, verify in Stape
5. Set Tagging Server URL in GTM
6. Configure GA4 client, test in DebugView

**Key gotchas:**
- Cloudflare proxy must be OFF for the subdomain verification (orange cloud → grey cloud)
- Subdomain naming matters: ad blockers maintain blocklists of obvious tracking subdomain names
- Stape EU region is critical for GDPR compliance (Frankfurt data center)

**Cost comparison:**
- Stape Basic ($20/mo) - typical KMU
- Stape Pro + Power-Ups ($50-150/mo) - larger shops with Meta + Google CAPI
- Self-hosted GCP Cloud Run ($10-30 + DevOps time)
- Platform app (Elevar/TrackBee, $99-200/mo)

---

## 5. Meta CAPI via Server-Side GTM (Cluster Article #2 Summary)

**Why Meta CAPI exists:** Meta Pixel alone captures only 50-60% of real conversions
in 2026 due to ad blockers, iOS ATT, Safari ITP. CAPI sends events server-side
direct to Meta - independent of browser.

**Event Match Quality (EMQ):** Meta scores each event 1-10 based on identifier
quality. EMQ ≥ 8.0 correlates with 15-25% better attributed conversions vs
EMQ ≤ 6.0.

**5-step setup:**
1. **Pixel config with event_id** - JavaScript Custom Variable generates unique event_id per conversion, passed to Facebook Pixel tag
2. **Meta tag in sGTM** - install CAPI tag (Stape community template or gtm-templates-simo-ahava), enter Pixel ID + Access Token + Test Event Code
3. **Hash PII** - SHA-256 hash email/phone, pass fbc/fbp cookies, set client_ip_address + client_user_agent from server request
4. **Verify deduplication** - identical event_id between Pixel and CAPI; check "Deduplicated" label in Events Manager Test Events
5. **EMQ check + optimization** - target EMQ ≥ 8.0; add city, state, zip, first_name, last_name, external_id (CRM ID)

**Most critical fields for EMQ:**
- Hashed email - moves score the most
- Hashed phone (E.164 format)
- fbc (Facebook Click ID), fbp (Facebook Browser ID) - NOT hashed
- client_ip_address, client_user_agent - NOT hashed

**GDPR compliance requirements:**
- Consent Mode v2 active (tags block without consent)
- SHA-256 for all PII before send
- DPA with Meta (Art. 28 GDPR)
- EU server region
- Never send raw unhashed PII

**Common issues:**
- Cookies (fbp, fbc) capped to 7 days by Safari ITP without Same-Origin sGTM - kills EMQ for Safari users
- Forgetting to hash some PII fields (only email but not phone) drops EMQ unnecessarily
- Test Event Code left in production sends events to test environment
- Double-hashing (manually hashing then letting Meta tag re-hash) breaks matching

---

## 6. Google Ads Enhanced Conversions (Cluster Article #3 Summary)

**Why Enhanced Conversions:** Classic GCLID-only tracking achieves 60-70% match
rate. Cookies break (Safari 7-day cap, ad blockers, iOS). Enhanced Conversions
add hash-matched user data to GCLID, raising match rate to 90%+.

**Quantified impact:**
- 5-10% more measured conversions average
- YouTube campaigns median +17% per Google's data
- B2B sales cycles with email-match: dramatic recovery of attribution

**5-step setup:**
1. **Conversion Linker in Web container** - All Pages trigger, captures GCLID, gclsrc, gbraid, wbraid into `_gcl_*` first-party cookies
2. **User data in dataLayer** - on conversion event push `user_data: {email, phone_number, address: {...}}` UNHASHED
3. **Google Ads Conversion tag in sGTM** - Conversion ID/Label, map user_data, trigger on event
4. **Activate Enhanced Conversions in Google Ads UI** - Tools > Conversions > select > Enhanced Conversions ON, Implementation: Google Tag, Privacy Policy URL required
5. **Match rate diagnostics** - target >70%, ideal 90%+; under 50% means broken data capture or wrong hash format

**Critical mistake (~30% of setups):**
- DO NOT hash user data yourself - Google's Conversion tag hashes automatically
- Double-hashing makes matching impossible
- This is OPPOSITE to Meta CAPI where you MUST hash yourself
- People running both setups frequently confuse this

**Field hierarchy by match impact:**
- Email - very high (80%+ of matches)
- Phone (E.164) - high
- First name + Last name - medium
- Address (street, city, region, postal_code, country) - low to medium

**Web vs Leads unification (April 2026):**
Until April 2026, Enhanced Conversions for Web and Enhanced Conversions for
Leads were separate settings. Since April 2026 they're unified - Google Ads
simultaneously accepts user data from website tags, Data Manager, and API
connections.

**Conversion Import for B2B (long sales cycles):**
- In CRM: on deal-closed capture email + GCLID
- Send to Google Ads via Data Manager (UI), Google Ads API (automated), or Zapier/Salesforce integration
- Google matches email against original click, attributes conversion back
- Essential for B2B SaaS with HubSpot/Pipedrive - Smart Bidding then optimizes for paying customers not just signups

---

## 7. Safari ITP Bypass via Same-Origin sGTM (Cluster Article #4 Summary)

**The problem:** Safari ITP has capped JavaScript cookies at 7 days since 2019.
Since Safari 16.4 (April 2023), the IP-Matching Rule also caps server-set cookies
to 7 days if the sGTM server IP doesn't match the main site IP prefix.

**Why standard Stape sGTM doesn't help against Safari ITP:**
- Subdomain (sgtm.example.com) on Stape's IP range
- Main site (example.com) on Cloudflare or other IP range
- Safari sees different IP prefixes → treats sGTM as "suspicious first-party" → caps cookies to 7 days
- 95% of sGTM setups out there hit this without realizing

**Why CNAME cloaking is dead since 2020:**
- Safari/WebKit detects when CNAME target is outside the eTLD+1
- Caps JavaScript cookies on cloaked subdomains to 7 days
- WebKit official statement, Nov 2020 blog post

**Business impact by sales cycle:**
- Small e-commerce (impulse buy, days): 3-5% loss
- Medium e-commerce (1-3 week comparison shopping): 10-15% loss
- SaaS trial → paid (2-4 weeks): 15-20% loss
- B2B lead-gen (1-6 months): 20-30% loss
- Local services (days): 2-3% loss

**Three solution paths:**
1. **HTTP Set-Cookie with IP match** - only viable for enterprise with own server infrastructure
2. **Same-Origin Path-Based sGTM** ⭐ - reverse proxy (Cloudflare Worker / Nginx) routes example.com/sgtm/* to Stape; same-origin = no IP matching issue = 400-day cookies
3. **Stape Custom Loader Power-Up** ($20/mo) - Stape injects loader script that routes through main domain; plug-and-play same-origin

**Cloudflare Worker Code Pattern:**
```javascript
export default {
  async fetch(request, env) {
    const url = new URL(request.url);
    const targetPath = url.pathname.replace(/^\/sgtm/, '');
    const targetUrl = 'https://sgtm.example.com' + targetPath + url.search;
    const proxyRequest = new Request(targetUrl, {
      method: request.method,
      headers: request.headers,
      body: request.body,
      redirect: 'manual'
    });
    const response = await fetch(proxyRequest);
    return new Response(response.body, {
      status: response.status,
      headers: response.headers  // critical: pass Set-Cookie through
    });
  }
};
```

**Verification - ITP Debug Mode:**
1. Safari Preferences > Advanced > Show Develop menu
2. Develop > Experimental Features > ITP Debug Mode
3. Develop > Show Web Inspector > Storage > Cookies - check Expires column (400 days, not 7)
4. Console tab: search "ITP Debug:" logs for capping reasons

**GDPR remains unchanged:** longer cookie lifetime does NOT make tracking
"more GDPR-compliant" - all standard requirements (Consent Mode v2, Privacy
Policy, DPA, data minimization) still apply.
